title: Pan-Seared Saudi Arabian Lamb Kebabs
description: A flavorful and aromatic lamb kebab dish inspired by Saudi Arabian cuisine, featuring tender lamb skewers seared to perfection in a pan.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: You can also use lamb neck or lamb shank.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount according to your preference.

ingredients.name: Coarse salt
ingredients.notes: You can also use sea salt or kosher salt.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount according to your preference.

ingredients.name: Ghee or vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount according to your preference.

ingredients.name: Onion, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: You can use red onion or yellow onion.

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount according to your preference.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley, chopped
ingredients.notes: You can use fresh cilantro instead.

ingredients.name: Fresh mint, chopped
ingredients.notes: You can use fresh cilantro instead.

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount according to your preference.

instructions: In a large bowl, combine lamb cubes, olive oil, coarse salt, and black pepper. Mix well until the lamb is evenly coated.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
Preheat a large non-stick pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the lamb from the refrigerator and thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Add the skewers to the preheated pan and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until the lamb is browned and cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the skewers to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
In the same pan, add ghee or vegetable oil and swirl to coat the bottom.
Add the sliced onion and cook for 3-4 minutes, or until softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the chopped parsley and mint, and cook for 1 minute, or until wilted.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the onion mixture and stir to combine.
Serve the lamb skewers with the onion mixture spooned over the top.
